meta.title: VibeCap — Screenshot Tool for Vibe Coding on macOS
meta.description: VibeCap is a macOS screenshot tool for vibe coding. Capture, annotate, and send screenshots with prompts to AI code editors like Cursor in one action. Free trial.

hero.headline: VibeCap — Screenshot Tool for Vibe Coding
hero.subheadline: A lightweight screenshot tool for macOS that helps you turn screenshots into AI-ready conversations — in one click.
hero.entityParagraph: VibeCap is a macOS screenshot tool designed for vibe coding workflows. It lets developers capture screen areas, add annotations, write prompts, and send everything to AI-powered code editors like Cursor, Windsurf, and GitHub Copilot in a single copy-paste action. Unlike traditional macOS screenshots that disappear before you can use them, VibeCap keeps captures in context. No accounts, no cloud dependency — runs entirely on your Mac.

problems.title: Why macOS Screenshots Slow Down Vibe Coding
problems.subtitle: macOS screenshots are powerful. But once you start vibe coding, they become friction.

items.title: The screenshot disappears before you're ready
items.description: You take a screenshot. If you don't grab it immediately and drop it into the input box in time, it's gone. Now you're digging through folders. That moment of flow is already broken.

items.title: Moving screenshots across windows is fragile
items.description: Even if you catch the screenshot, you still have to drag it across screens, find the right window, aim for the right input box. Miss once, and you're retrying.

items.title: Multiple screenshots mean repeated pain
items.description: Need to send two or three screenshots together? You repeat the same steps again and again: capture → grab → drag → drop. It's slow. And it pulls you out of your thinking every time.

items.title: Annotation exists, but doesn't help much
items.description: macOS annotation tools technically work. But they're not designed for explaining problems to an editor: switching tools, clicking tiny buttons, marking without context.

solution.title: How VibeCap Works
solution.subtitle: VibeCap is not a replacement for your editor. It fixes the moment before you talk to it.

flow.step4: Send everything to your editor — once

benefits: No repeating the same action three times

workflow.title: Screenshot to AI Editor in Seconds
workflow.description: VibeCap understands how vibe coding actually works: You don't want to explain everything. You want to show, then say a few words.
workflow.note: At the moment you're still in context.

items.title: Prompt + image, sent together
points: Image(s) and text go into the editor at the same time

items.title: Editor-friendly by design
points: Works naturally with modern coding tools

pricing.title: VibeCap Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Pick the plan that fits how you work.
pricing.description: No accounts. Cancel anytime for subscriptions.
pricing.footnote: No feature differences. Just different ways to pay.

closing.title: Download VibeCap — Free on the Mac App Store
closing.description: It doesn't replace your editor or your AI tools. It simply removes friction from the capture → prompt → continue loop.
closing.highlight: From everyday AI tasks to vibe coding workflows, VibeCap helps your screenshots speak clearly to AI.

items.question: What is VibeCap?
items.answer: VibeCap is a macOS menu-bar screenshot tool designed for vibe coding. It allows developers to capture screen areas, add annotations, write prompts, and send screenshots directly to AI-powered code editors such as Cursor, Windsurf, and GitHub Copilot — all in a single copy-paste action.

items.question: How does VibeCap work?
items.answer: VibeCap runs in the macOS menu bar. Users trigger a capture via a global keyboard shortcut (default: ⌘⇧C), select a screen area, optionally annotate the screenshot and write a prompt, then copy everything. VibeCap automatically pastes both the image and the text into the target editor when the user presses ⌘V.

items.question: What platforms does VibeCap support?
items.answer: VibeCap is a native macOS application. It requires macOS 13.0 (Ventura) or later and supports both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs. There is currently no Windows or Linux version.

items.question: Is VibeCap free to use?
items.answer: VibeCap offers a free tier that includes area capture, screenshot saving, and arrow annotations. A Pro subscription unlocks additional annotation tools (rectangles, circles, numbered markers) and color customization. Plans start at $1.99/month, $17.99/year, or a one-time $24.99 lifetime purchase.

items.question: Does VibeCap collect any personal data?
items.answer: VibeCap does not collect, store, transmit, or share any personal data. All screenshots and prompts remain on the user's device. VibeCap does not include analytics, tracking, advertising SDKs, or cloud services. It operates entirely offline.

items.question: How does VibeCap compare to macOS built-in screenshots?
items.answer: macOS built-in screenshots (⌘⇧3/4) capture images that disappear from the preview quickly, require manual dragging across windows, and lack prompt-attachment support. VibeCap keeps screenshots in context, supports multiple captures per session, provides annotation tools designed for explaining code problems, and delivers screenshots with prompts to the editor in one action.

items.question: Which code editors work with VibeCap?
items.answer: VibeCap works with any application that accepts pasted images. It is optimized for AI-powered code editors including Cursor, Windsurf, GitHub Copilot, and other tools that accept screenshot-based prompts. It also works with chat applications like Slack, Discord, and web-based AI interfaces.

items.question: Does VibeCap require an internet connection?
items.answer: VibeCap does not require an internet connection for its core functionality. All captures, annotations, and clipboard operations happen locally on the device. An internet connection is only needed for initial App Store download and in-app purchase verification.

items.question: What annotation tools does VibeCap provide?
items.answer: VibeCap provides arrow, rectangle, circle, and numbered-marker annotation tools. The free tier includes arrows in red. Pro users can access all annotation shapes and choose from six colors: red, blue, green, yellow, orange, and purple.

items.question: Can VibeCap send multiple screenshots at once?
items.answer: Yes. VibeCap supports capturing multiple screenshots in a single session. Users can take several captures, annotate each one individually, then write a single prompt and send all images together to their editor in one paste action.

items.question: How does VibeCap compare to CopyCut and SnapCode?
items.answer: VibeCap, CopyCut, and SnapCode all serve the AI coding screenshot workflow but differ in approach. CopyCut focuses on copying file paths for reference in prompts. SnapCode is built specifically for Claude Code integration. VibeCap combines multi-screenshot capture, visual annotations, prompt text attachment, and auto-paste into any AI editor in a single flow — no file path management required.

items.question: How does VibeCap compare to CleanShot X for AI coding workflows?
items.answer: CleanShot X is a comprehensive screenshot and screen recording tool with 50+ features including cloud storage, scrolling capture, and GIF recording. However, it does not support prompt text attachment or auto-paste to AI code editors. VibeCap is purpose-built for the capture-annotate-prompt-paste workflow that AI-assisted coding demands, with no unnecessary features adding complexity.

items.question: Can VibeCap be used for non-coding tasks?
items.answer: Yes. While VibeCap is optimized for AI code editors like Cursor, Windsurf, and GitHub Copilot, it works with any application that accepts pasted images. Users regularly use VibeCap to send annotated screenshots to ChatGPT, Claude, Slack, Discord, Notion, and other tools where visual context improves communication.

footer.description: A screenshot tool built specifically for vibe coding.

meta.title: Privacy Policy — VibeCap
meta.description: VibeCap Privacy Policy - Learn how we protect your data

overview.title: Overview
content: VibeCap is a macOS menu bar application designed to help users capture screenshots and quickly paste them into other applications, such as code editors or chat tools.
**VibeCap does not collect, store, transmit, or share any personal data.**
All operations are performed locally on your device.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection
dataCollection.intro: **We do not collect any personal information or usage data.**
items: We do **not** collect text input or prompts
We do **not** use analytics, tracking, or advertising SDKs
We do **not** use cookies or identifiers
dataCollection.footer: VibeCap does not communicate with any external servers.

screenContent.title: Screen Content
screenContent.intro: When you take a screenshot using VibeCap:
items: The captured image exists only in memory on your device
The image is optionally saved **only** to a folder you explicitly choose
The image is pasted directly into the target application you select
screenContent.footer: VibeCap never uploads or transmits screenshots outside your device.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.intro: VibeCap requires certain macOS system permissions to function correctly:

screenRecording.title: Screen Recording
screenRecording.description: Used solely to capture the screen area you select when taking a screenshot.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: Used to simulate keyboard actions (such as paste commands) in the target application you choose.

notes: Used only for the described functionality
Never used to monitor user behavior

localStorage.title: Local Storage
localStorage.intro: VibeCap stores limited configuration data locally using macOS system storage, including:
items: App settings such as auto-save and launch-at-login
localStorage.footer: This data never leaves your device.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.intro: VibeCap does not integrate with any third-party services.

dataSharing.title: Data Sharing
dataSharing.content: Because VibeCap does not collect data, **no data is shared with third parties**.

childrenPrivacy.title: Children's Privacy
childrenPrivacy.content: VibeCap does not collect any data and is safe for users of all ages.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.content: If VibeCap's functionality changes in a way that affects privacy, this Privacy Policy will be updated accordingly.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact:

The App operates entirely locally and does not provide cloud-based services.
